description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped stone with a predominantly gray coloration and a rough, natural texture. One side of the stone features a distinct fossil-like imprint, possibly of a marine organism, adding a unique visual element. The stone's surfaces are uneven, with visible cracks and subtle color variations ranging from light to dark gray. On one narrow edge, there is a handwritten alphanumeric marking, suggesting cataloging or identification, likely for scientific or archaeological purposes. The stone appears dense and solid, with no visible cavities or openings. Its overall appearance suggests it has been exposed to natural weathering, giving it a slightly worn and aged look.
description: A rough, gray stone with an irregular shape, featuring a fossil-like imprint on one side and catalog markings on an edge. The surface is weathered and solid, with visible cracks and color variations.
description_view_0: The broad, rough side of the stone is visible, showing natural texture and subtle color gradations.
description_view_1: A narrow edge is shown, displaying a handwritten catalog number and a relatively smooth, lighter surface.
description_view_2: This side reveals a prominent fossil-like imprint, with swirling lines and a central star-shaped pattern.
description_view_3: Another narrow edge is visible, similar in texture to view 1 but without any markings.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or used as a decorative obstacle
attribute_pattern: Fossil-like imprint with swirling lines and a central star shape on one side; otherwise, irregular mottling and natural cracks.
attribute_weathering: Rough and slightly worn, with visible cracks and surface aging indicative of prolonged exposure to the elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with manicured lawns or paved surfaces, but can blend with other natural stones or landscaping elements.
